package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for fmt_sprintf_float_to_string: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `fmt.Sprintf("%f", ...)` instead of `strconv.FormatFloat(...)` when only a float string is needed.
type CaseFmtSprintfFloatToStringDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseFmtSprintfFloatToStringModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseFmtSprintfFloatToStringRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseFmtSprintfFloatToStringModel
}

type CaseFmtSprintfFloatToStringService struct {
    repo *CaseFmtSprintfFloatToStringRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseFmtSprintfFloatToStringAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveFmtSprintfFloatToString(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseFmtSprintfFloatToStringDTO) error {
    focus := "fmt_sprintf_float_to_string"
    _ = focus
    someFloat := 3.14
    str := fmt.Sprintf("%f", someFloat)
    _ = str
    return nil
}
